pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014
Case study: client cannot lose weight

•
Full legal name: Kevin
•
Full billing address:
•
Age: 43
•
Height: 183cm
•
Weight: 92.8kg
•
Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): 20% using bio impedance
inbody570 machine.
This suggests Kevin would be in contest shape at around 79 kg, a competitive weight at his
height. Given his novice strength level below, it is more likely that bioelectrical impedance
analysis underestimated his fat percentage, but since we don't have any pictures, let's use
20%.
•
Years of training experience: 2-3
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in weight times reps, e.g. 250 lb x
6.
•
Bench press: 70k x 5
•
Squat (excluding body weight): 80k x 5
•
Chin-up (excluding body weight): 5 x body weight only
•
Overhead press: 45k x 5

What are your fitness goals? Wants to be maximum muscular hypertrophy naturally with a
body fat of around 12%. Especially interested in upper body hypertrophy.

How often in a week would you be prepared to train? Note that no session should last longer
than an hour; 2 sessions is maintenance, 3 is doable and twice daily would be the absolute
maximum at which more is no longer better.
3 days per week in the gym.

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014
What is your occupation? You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal
trainer/manual labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm, stress and
activity level.
Manager at a bank.

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies, illnesses, syndromes, disorders
etc.) you have or had:
Has lower back trouble. Told by physio it is not a disc problem. Comes and goes. Usually is
worse after travelling on long flights, which is only once every 2 months.
Traveling long distance is a red flag combined with subpar compliance or an ad libitum diet
(described below). The stress and difficulty in maintaining a consistent training program almost
invariably lead to fat gain.

Do you plan to use any PEDs while under my supervision? If so, please list them:

No

Activity level: please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
Somewhat active (e.g. doing groceries or walking your dog but otherwise sedentary) Y
Stress level: please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
Average stress (e.g. full-time work with deadlines and commuting) Y

Please list all the supplements you are currently taking:
Whey protein, cod liver oil, multi vit,

Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:

Maxmuscle cyclone whey protein.
7am - actimel yoghurt drink, low fat fruit yoghurt
7.30am - medium banana, cup of black tea
10am - cup of green tea
Midday - chicken noodle salad and medium skimmed milk latte
2pm - Goberry shake with 1 scoop Maxmuscle cyclone whey protein powder
3pm - cup of green tea and 300mls of water
4pm - trail mix granola bar
6pm - beef lasagne and baked beans
8pm - protein shake (400mls skimmed milk and 1 scoops maxi muscle cyclone)
9pm - 300mls water with fruit cordial

8 meals a day, this is one of the first priorities to correct.

7th Jan

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014

7am - actimel yoghurt drink and low fat fruit yoghurt
7.30am - medium sized banana, cup of black tea, 350mls maxi-muscle cyclone protein shake
(1 scoops)
10am - cup of black tea and a trail mix granola bar
12.30 - ceasar salad with grilled salmon, small brown bread roll. 500mls water
3pm - skimmed milk latte
4pm - cup of black tea
5.30pm - roast beef, broccoli, carrot, swede, parsnips, roast potatoes.
6pm - 300mls water
9pm - protein shake, 300mls skimmed milk and 1 scoops protein

Now only 7 meals: his meal frequency is not only possibly too high, it is also inconsistent.

8th Jan

7am - actimel yoghurt drink, low fat fruit yoghurt, protein shake - 350mls water and 1 scoops
powder.
10am - small latte, fresh strawberries
1.15pm - large Goberry shake with 1 scoops protein
2.30 - chicken Caesar salad, cup of black tea, 300mls water
3pm - medium banana
4pm - cup of black tea, 300mls of water.
6pm - Shepherds Pie with garden peas (lamb mince).
6.30pm - 330mls water
9pm - protein shake - 400mls skimmed milk, 1 scoops protein.
Client not counting macros but is willing to start now.

Kevin isn't tracking his macros while eating a subpar diet with meals that don't contain protein or
consist entirely of supplements or processed food more or less around the clock. It is not surprising
then that he isn't losing weight. In this case, I highly recommend having Kevin track his macros for at
least 2 weeks. He's already logging his meals, so as his trainer you may even offer to calculate his
macros for now and give him feedback. Kevin needs to learn how many calories are in 'innocent'
meals like Goberry shakes and a latte (if it's from Starbucks, there could be several hundred calories
in it), not to mention Shepherd's Pie.
Please describe or attach your current training program in detail:
Trains with weights 3 days per week. Full body each day. One day 8-10 reps bodybuilding
exercises, 1 exercise per muscle group 4 sets. One day strength 3-6 reps 3-5 sets per exercise.
One day higher reps of 10-15 reps, 4 sets per exercise, one exercise per muscle, with overload
techniques added (eg. Rest pause and drop sets).
Some cycling outside of the gym. A few miles 1-2 times per week.

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014
Let's create a diet for Kevin based on the above information. Compliance wise, I would
recommend prescribing a full meal plan for Kevin with food choices that differ completely
from his regular diet. This will open up his mind to a more regular fitness diet with protein
at every meal and less processed foods.

Kevin's genetic indicators were at least average, so it's likely that his 3 years in the gym
simply weren't spent productively, based on his strength level, and we can estimate that his
anabolic window is about 24 h. However, to differentiate this case study from the previous
one, let's assume Kevin has poor genetic capacity to build muscle and he is now already
relatively advanced with an anabolic window of 16 h.

This also has implications for his diet: despite his fat percentage of 20%, his poor
bodybuilding genetics and relatively advanced training status make it unwise to use a deficit
of more than 20%, so we'll use 20%.

Let's also postulate he can only train after work and needs to go to bed early, leaving him
with only enough time to consume 1 large meal between his work-out and going to bed.

This leads to the following diet.

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014
